campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including investment accounts and spending. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at how people can get around using different methods like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and money management reports
City treasurer's monthly financial summary
New multimodal transportation plan to improve how residents travel around Campbell

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could comment on a new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council introduced a new ordinance and decided to skip the normal first reading process to speed things up, planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced (specific topic not specified in agenda)
First reading waived to expedite approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused mainly on administrative housekeeping from previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six separate March meetings
Construction contract with QLM, Inc. (details incomplete in agenda)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction contract
Mix of open and closed session meetings reviewed

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council reviewed several reports on programs helping young people in the community. They discussed youth services plans, summer safety programs, and how the library is working to improve its facilities and services for residents.
keyTopics: Progress update on the city's plan for children and youth services
Safe Summer Initiative grant to keep kids engaged during summer months
Youth intervention and empowerment programs in San José
Code enforcement activities across the city
Library facility improvements and customer experience updates

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and virtual participation options. Residents were able to join online via Zoom or phone to watch the proceedings and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures (raise hand feature or *9 on phone)
Instructions for unmuting (*6 on phone to speak)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their April 9th meeting at 7 P.M., where they started with the Pledge of Allegiance and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission monthly meeting
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Pledge of Allegiance and flag salute

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The main topics included a food-related program called Budget and Bites, a youth organization called Unmuted Tabs, and job opportunities for young people through the city's Summer Jobs program. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous session in March.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino Summer Jobs opportunities
Approval of March 11, 2026 meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using a hybrid format that allowed residents to attend in person or join remotely. The city provided multiple ways for residents to participate, including Zoom video conferencing and phone dial-in options, as well as the ability to submit written comments before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote attendance available)
Zoom participation: Meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Phone dial-in option at 1(669) 900-6833
Written public comments can be submitted via eComment on the City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including reviewing updates to the city's federal funding plans for 2025-2026, looking at new project requests from individual council members, and considering changes to how council vacancies are filled. The meeting also included routine items like approving previous meeting minutes and planning the calendar for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to federal funding and consolidated development plan for 2025-2026
New project requests from individual council members
Proposed changes to council vacancy filling procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the city's gas utility financial plan for the next fiscal year, which included adjusting gas rates for residential and commercial customers. The council also approved updated financial reserves and transfers to cover utility operations and maintenance.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates going up for residential customers (Schedule G-1)
Commercial gas service rates adjusted (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
Financial reserves and budget transfers approved for gas utility operations
Meeting materials added late to the agenda for council review
